onboarding_gps_heading: Before you start: enable location on your camera
onboarding_gps_intro: Your photos need GPS coordinates so we can put them on the map. Here's how to make sure your camera saves location data:
onboarding_gps_footer: Once enabled, every photo you take will include GPS automatically. You only need to do this once.
onboarding_gps_privacy_note: If you don't want your other photos to be geotagged, remember to turn Location Services off again afterwards.
onboarding_gps_iphone_5: Recommended: Settings → Camera → Formats → select Most Compatible (uses JPG instead of HEIC)
onboarding_gps_android_1: Turn on Location — pull down from the top of your screen and tap the Location icon to turn it on. (Or: Settings → Location → turn ON)
onboarding_gps_android_4: Find Location tags, GPS tags, or Save location → turn ON
onboarding_gps_android_upload_tip: When selecting photos to upload, tap ⋮ in the top-right of the file picker and choose Browse instead of selecting from Photos or Albums. The default Android picker can strip GPS data from your photos.
Open Settings → Privacy & Security → Loc: Open Settings → Privacy & Security → Location Services
